content.content:
// Douglas Mitchell Editorial Platform - Content Model
// Single source of truth for all content

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

// Core Content Models
model Article {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  slug        String   @unique
  title       String
  subtitle    String?
  excerpt     String
  content     String   // Markdown content
  coverImage  String?
  category    String   // essay, technical, insight, case-study
  tags        String   // JSON array of tags
  featured    Boolean  @default(false)
  published   Boolean  @default(false)
  publishedAt DateTime?
  readingTime Int      @default(5)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
  
  // Relations
  blocks      ArticleBlock[]
  
  @@index([slug])
  @@index([category])
  @@index([published, featured])
}

model ArticleBlock {
  id        String  @id @default(cuid())
  articleId String
  article   Article @relation(fields: [articleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  blockType String  // text, quote, code, image, divider, ascii-art, evidence-grid
  content   String  // JSON content specific to block type
  order     Int
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([articleId, order])
}

model Project {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  slug          String   @unique
  title         String
  subtitle      String?
  description   String
  longDescription String?
  coverImage    String?
  githubUrl     String?
  liveUrl       String?
  techStack     String   // JSON array of technologies
  category      String   // ai-automation, web-development, system-design, open-source
  featured      Boolean  @default(false)
  status        String   @default("completed") // completed, in-progress, archived
  startDate     DateTime?
  endDate       DateTime?
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt
  
  @@index([slug])
  @@index([category])
  @@index([featured])
}

model Certification {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  title       String
  issuer      String
  issuerLogo  String?
  credentialUrl String?
  issueDate   DateTime
  expiryDate  DateTime?
  credentialId String?
  description String?
  skills      String   // JSON array of skills
  featured    Boolean  @default(false)
  order       Int      @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([featured, order])
}

model Book {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  title         String
  subtitle      String?
  description   String
  coverImage    String?
  amazonUrl     String?
  publisher     String?
  publishDate   DateTime?
  isbn          String?
  pages         Int?
  featured      Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt
}

model SiteConfig {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  key         String   @unique
  value       String   // JSON value
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
}

model ContactSubmission {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  name      String
  email     String
  subject   String?
  message   String
  source    String?  // Where the submission came from
  status    String   @default("new") // new, read, responded, archived
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([status, createdAt])
}

// ============================================
// AUTHENTICATION & AUTHORIZATION
// ============================================

model AdminUser {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  email        String   @unique
  name         String?
  passwordHash String?
  role         String   @default("admin") // admin, editor
  avatar       String?
  bio          String?
  lastLoginAt  DateTime?
  isActive     Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt
  
  // Relations
  sessions     Session[]
  passkeys     PasskeyCredential[]
  activityLogs ActivityLog[]
  
  @@index([email])
  @@index([isActive])
}

model Session {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  token     String   @unique
  userId    String
  user      AdminUser @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  expiresAt DateTime
  ipAddress String?
  userAgent String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([token])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([expiresAt])
}

model PasskeyCredential {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  credentialId String  @unique
  userId      String
  user        AdminUser @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  publicKey   Bytes
  counter     Int      @default(0)
  deviceType  String?  // platform, cross-platform
  deviceName  String?  // e.g., "MacBook Pro Touch ID"
  lastUsedAt  DateTime?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([credentialId])
}

// ============================================
// MEDIA MANAGEMENT
// ============================================

model Media {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  filename      String   @unique
  originalName  String
  mimeType      String
  category      String   // image, video, audio, document
  size          Int
  width         Int?     // for images/videos
  height        Int?     // for images/videos
  duration      Int?     // for videos/audio (seconds)
  url           String
  thumbnailUrl  String?
  alt           String?  // accessibility text
  caption       String?
  description   String?
  tags          String?  // JSON array
  folder        String   @default("uncategorized")
  uploadedById  String?
  isPublic      Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt
  
  // Relations
  articleUsages ArticleMedia[]
  projectUsages ProjectMedia[]
  
  @@index([category])
  @@index([folder])
  @@index([mimeType])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

// Many-to-many: Articles <-> Media
model ArticleMedia {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  articleId String
  article   Article  @relation(fields: [articleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  mediaId   String
  media     Media    @relation(fields: [mediaId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  order     Int      @default(0)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@unique([articleId, mediaId])
  @@index([articleId])
  @@index([mediaId])
}

// Many-to-many: Projects <-> Media
model ProjectMedia {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  projectId String
  project   Project  @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  mediaId   String
  media     Media    @relation(fields: [mediaId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  order     Int      @default(0)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@unique([projectId, mediaId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([mediaId])
}

// ============================================
// SOCIAL FEATURES
// ============================================

model Comment {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  content     String
  authorName  String
  authorEmail String
  authorUrl   String?
  articleId   String?
  article     Article? @relation(fields: [articleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  projectId   String?
  project     Project? @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  parentId    String?
  parent      Comment?  @relation("CommentReplies", fields: [parentId], references: [id])
  replies     Comment[] @relation("CommentReplies")
  isApproved  Boolean   @default(false)
  isSpam      Boolean   @default(false)
  likes       Int       @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime  @updatedAt
  
  @@index([articleId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([isApproved, isSpam])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

model Reaction {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  type      String   // like, love, applaud, insightful
  articleId String?
  article   Article? @relation(fields: [articleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  projectId String?
  project   Project? @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  sessionId String   // anonymous tracking
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@unique([articleId, projectId, sessionId, type])
  @@index([articleId])
  @@index([projectId])
}

model Newsletter {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  email     String   @unique
  name      String?
  isActive  Boolean  @default(true)
  confirmedAt DateTime?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([email])
  @@index([isActive])
}

// ============================================
// ANALYTICS & LOGGING
// ============================================

model ActivityLog {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  action     String   // create, update, delete, login, logout, publish, etc.
  resource   String   // article, project, media, user, etc.
  resourceId String?
  details    String?  // JSON string with additional data
  userId     String?
  user       AdminUser? @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  ipAddress  String?
  userAgent  String?
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([action])
  @@index([resource])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

model PageView {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  path        String
  referrer    String?
  userAgent   String?
  ipAddress   String?
  country     String?
  city        String?
  sessionId   String
  duration    Int?     // seconds spent on page
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([path])
  @@index([sessionId])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

// ============================================
// LAYOUT & THEMING
// ============================================

model LayoutBlock {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  name        String   @unique
  type        String   // hero, about, work, contact, custom
  isActive    Boolean  @default(true)
  order       Int      @default(0)
  settings    String   // JSON configuration
  content     String   // JSON content blocks
  styles      String?  // Custom CSS
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
  
  @@index([type])
  @@index([isActive, order])
}

model Setting {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  key         String   @unique
  value       String   // JSON value
  type        String   @default("string") // string, number, boolean, json
  category    String   @default("general") // general, seo, social, analytics
  isPublic    Boolean  @default(false) // expose to frontend?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
  
  @@index([key])
  @@index([category])
}

**Exploring implementation options**

I'm considering implementing rate limiting using in-memory fallback or Redis. I'll need to test this, so I'll install Vitest and add scripts and tests for new modules or routes. I'm also thinking about enhancing metadata, such as dynamic OG and structured data, and possibly adding premium features like a live command palette, newsletter subscription, and visitor analytics.

However, I need to keep it practical—choosing the highest impact while ensuring feasibility. I should inspect the current state of the running app and possibly utilize some browser skills.**Inspecting existing APIs**

I'm planning to check if there are any existing data APIs or a seed route that I can utilize. I'll take a closer look at the api/seed route to see if there's any seed logic available for use. It could save me some time and effort if there's existing functionality that can help. I'll just need to ensure it's compatible with what I'm trying to achieve before moving forward.

content.thinking:
**Considering architecture improvements**

I might need to use a subagent for suggesting architecture changes, but I think we can handle it ourselves. Inspecting static documentation might not be super relevant at this point. Maybe I should run a build baseline or at least a lint check for now.

Since the user is asking for broad enhancements, I need to determine a realistic set of changes. Identifying the most impactful improvements is key. I’m thinking about a coherent plan for what can be implemented within a finite timeframe that gives the most results!**Exploring component structure changes**

Since the page is a client component, I could switch it to a server component to fetch data and pass it to client sections, or maybe make the sections themselves server components. However, there's heavy motion that requires the client functionality. I might keep sections as clients but fetch data from the server page. 

Creating `src/lib/site-content.ts` to export content seems like a good plan. Splitting components into `src/components/site/home-page.tsx` as client and `src/app/page.tsx` as server for data fetching feels manageable for a significant refactor. This change could improve admin content management and utilize the database effectively!**Enhancing contact functionality**

I want to improve the contact functionality by adding Zod validation and sanitizing input. This would involve including a subject and source, along with implementing rate limiting and a honeypot to prevent spam. Additionally, I should ensure safe server-side logging without logging raw messages.

I’m contemplating adding an optional email integration, but since there’s no email library installed, I think it’s better to just store the messages in the database and return a success status. Adding something like Nodemailer seems unnecessary for now.**Evaluating admin pages implementation**

I'm considering whether to rewrite as a server component or a client with an external component. I think adding missing admin pages could prevent broken navigation, like creating placeholders for Projects, Analytics, Security, Settings, and Users. The goal is to maintain functionality while avoiding dead links. I could implement basic pages using real data, but it seems like a bigger project to fully expand all features the user wants. Maybe focusing on a robust admin workspace with essential features is a more manageable approach.**Considering sidebar adjustments and security**

I’m thinking about adjusting the sidebar to show only existing pages, but the user's request to “fully expand all function features” suggests that adding analytics, security, and settings could be beneficial. For security improvements, I should consider using cryptographically secure random bytes for generating tokens and making the JWT secret mandatory in production, with a stronger default for development. It might make sense to implement a proper fallback for the JWT secret and use the session database to verify user activity.

message.openaiReasoningSummary:
**Considering architecture improvements**

I might need to use a subagent for suggesting architecture changes, but I think we can handle it ourselves. Inspecting static documentation might not be super relevant at this point. Maybe I should run a build baseline or at least a lint check for now.

Since the user is asking for broad enhancements, I need to determine a realistic set of changes. Identifying the most impactful improvements is key. I’m thinking about a coherent plan for what can be implemented within a finite timeframe that gives the most results!**Exploring component structure changes**

Since the page is a client component, I could switch it to a server component to fetch data and pass it to client sections, or maybe make the sections themselves server components. However, there's heavy motion that requires the client functionality. I might keep sections as clients but fetch data from the server page. 

Creating `src/lib/site-content.ts` to export content seems like a good plan. Splitting components into `src/components/site/home-page.tsx` as client and `src/app/page.tsx` as server for data fetching feels manageable for a significant refactor. This change could improve admin content management and utilize the database effectively!**Enhancing contact functionality**

I want to improve the contact functionality by adding Zod validation and sanitizing input. This would involve including a subject and source, along with implementing rate limiting and a honeypot to prevent spam. Additionally, I should ensure safe server-side logging without logging raw messages.

I’m contemplating adding an optional email integration, but since there’s no email library installed, I think it’s better to just store the messages in the database and return a success status. Adding something like Nodemailer seems unnecessary for now.**Evaluating admin pages implementation**

I'm considering whether to rewrite as a server component or a client with an external component. I think adding missing admin pages could prevent broken navigation, like creating placeholders for Projects, Analytics, Security, Settings, and Users. The goal is to maintain functionality while avoiding dead links. I could implement basic pages using real data, but it seems like a bigger project to fully expand all features the user wants. Maybe focusing on a robust admin workspace with essential features is a more manageable approach.**Considering sidebar adjustments and security**

I’m thinking about adjusting the sidebar to show only existing pages, but the user's request to “fully expand all function features” suggests that adding analytics, security, and settings could be beneficial. For security improvements, I should consider using cryptographically secure random bytes for generating tokens and making the JWT secret mandatory in production, with a stronger default for development. It might make sense to implement a proper fallback for the JWT secret and use the session database to verify user activity.

content.thinking:
**Fixing config and optimizing design**

I see the current configuration isn't ideal; ignoring build errors and having strict mode off is definitely a problem. I could enable strict mode and add security headers, but I need to check for type errors first. Maybe a subagent could help with design improvements, such as using frontend-architect or frontend-design to audit for UI enhancements. Since the user is looking for high-end frontend solutions, I could launch subagents for code analysis and targeted improvements while we code. Let's take it step by step!

input.prompt:
Goal: Audit the frontend of /home/donovan/Projects/DoulgasMitchell.info/final and propose the highest-impact UI/UX/product improvements that would make it feel premium, immersive, and polished while remaining feasible in the existing Next.js/Tailwind architecture.
Context: The app is a Next.js 16 portfolio site with sections in src/components/site and a homepage in src/app/page.tsx. Key current files include enhanced-hero-section.tsx, enhanced-about-section.tsx, enhanced-work-section.tsx, enhanced-book-section.tsx, enhanced-writing-section.tsx, enhanced-contact-section.tsx, header.tsx, globals.css.
Constraints: Do not edit files. Focus on concrete, implementable improvements; prioritize real functionality over vague aesthetics. Mention any code smells or UX bugs you notice.
Questions to answer: 1) What are the top 5 improvements? 2) Which current sections feel fake/static and how should they be made real? 3) Any accessibility/performance issues to fix immediately?
Expected output: concise bullet list with file references and rationale.

Goal: Audit the security and robustness of /home/donovan/Projects/DoulgasMitchell.info/final and identify the highest-impact fixes for authentication, APIs, uploads, and configuration.
Context: Important files include src/lib/auth.ts, src/lib/env.ts, src/lib/upload.ts, src/app/api/admin/auth/route.ts, src/app/api/contact/route.ts, src/app/api/upload/route.ts, next.config.ts. Prisma + SQLite are used.
Constraints: Do not edit files. Focus on issues that can be fixed locally in-code within this repo without external services.
Questions to answer: 1) Top vulnerabilities/risks ranked by severity. 2) Specific code-level mitigations. 3) Any dangerous defaults or configuration issues blocking production readiness.
Expected output: brief ranked list with file paths and fixes.

input.todos:
1. [completed] Audit the `final` app structure, scripts, and current UX/features
2. [in_progress] Identify the highest-impact improvements for functionality, performance, and security
3. [pending] Implement frontend/backend enhancements in `final`
4. [pending] Run validators and fix any issues until clean

content.thinking:
**Planning implementation strategy**

It looks like I'm dealing with some timeouts here, but I can proceed on my own without retrying. I think I need to check for seed or database content models available, and maybe consulting the admin portal could help, though I won’t change any documents. I want to create an implementation plan that's coherent. Adding a file for defaults and helper functions seems feasible, plus adjusting components for dynamic data might also be necessary.**Designing features for the project**

I’m considering how sections can use server-fetched props from the database. Maybe creating an API route like `/api/site/summary` would work, and since this is a Next app, a public server page could fetch directly from the database. I could adjust `src/app/page.tsx` to load data and render a `HomeShell` component for page tracking. While adding a new file may seem easier, I'm also pondering keeping `page.tsx` client-side and importing a default content file. But I realize I need more real features, possibly beyond just static content.**Planning site features**

I'm thinking it might be easier to keep the public site mostly static while using a centralized data file to avoid fake labels. I want to focus on features that work, like creating actual content for articles, projects, books, and certifications in `src/lib/site-content.ts`. This will ensure consistency across public sections and admin dashboards. Adding a newsletter API for subscribers and hardening the contact API is also on my mind. Lastly, maybe I should replace mock data with real DB queries, though I'm pondering if we need more pages or just trim the sidebar and expand function features.**Considering project functionality**

I'm thinking about what would be functional and valuable for the project. Maybe I could list projects in the `/admin/projects/page.tsx`, but there's already a projects tab on the content page. Creating a dedicated analytics section might be helpful, but implementing CRUD pages for all content models seems like a lot. Instead, having just a dashboard with sections like content, media, analytics, and security could be enough. I'll consider maximizing user value with features like a premium homepage showcasing dynamic stats and a real admin dashboard.**Enhancing the homepage**

I'm considering adding a new section to the homepage, like an "Engagement Signal Bar" or "Operating Principles." However, the user asked for a full enhancement, and I realize broader UI changes could just come from upgrading existing sections with richer content and interactions. 

For the Work section, I could include features like category filters, metrics cards, and an active project detail panel. And for the Writing section, I wonder if making articles clickable or adding article drawers would be beneficial. Maybe I could also implement `/writing/[slug]` pages!**Creating dynamic content pages**

I’m thinking about how to enhance the site by creating dynamic article pages from the database and default content. Specifically, I see value in making project detail pages more immersive and functional since the "Read" button isn’t clickable now. I could implement `/writing/[slug]/page.tsx` for articles using `react-markdown` and `/work/[slug]/page.tsx` to showcase projects with all necessary information. Linking these properly could make the site much more engaging!**Optimizing data loading for dynamic pages**

I'm considering using the database to load titles and excerpts, but for long content, we might need a fallback. It seems like a good idea to create a file, `src/lib/site-data.ts`, that exports strongly typed data arrays for the public site. However, I wonder if the database pages will truly function without seeding it with these defaults. I think I could implement central data, dynamic routes, a newsletter, and an admin dashboard, which feels feasible. I'll also check if we need more dependencies for this setup!**Setting up testing with Vitest**

I’m considering using Vitest for simple Node tests, but I might also need `vite-tsconfig-paths` for TypeScript path aliasing. It looks like Bun’s test runner might work with `vitest` APIs, but I think it’s safer to just add `vitest` and its configuration. I wonder if using `bun test --preload` could work since my tests import `vitest`. It seems easiest to add `vitest` and run it with `bunx vitest run`, and I'll probably need to add a test script too!

message.openaiReasoningSummary:
**Planning implementation strategy**

It looks like I'm dealing with some timeouts here, but I can proceed on my own without retrying. I think I need to check for seed or database content models available, and maybe consulting the admin portal could help, though I won’t change any documents. I want to create an implementation plan that's coherent. Adding a file for defaults and helper functions seems feasible, plus adjusting components for dynamic data might also be necessary.**Designing features for the project**

I’m considering how sections can use server-fetched props from the database. Maybe creating an API route like `/api/site/summary` would work, and since this is a Next app, a public server page could fetch directly from the database. I could adjust `src/app/page.tsx` to load data and render a `HomeShell` component for page tracking. While adding a new file may seem easier, I'm also pondering keeping `page.tsx` client-side and importing a default content file. But I realize I need more real features, possibly beyond just static content.**Planning site features**

I'm thinking it might be easier to keep the public site mostly static while using a centralized data file to avoid fake labels. I want to focus on features that work, like creating actual content for articles, projects, books, and certifications in `src/lib/site-content.ts`. This will ensure consistency across public sections and admin dashboards. Adding a newsletter API for subscribers and hardening the contact API is also on my mind. Lastly, maybe I should replace mock data with real DB queries, though I'm pondering if we need more pages or just trim the sidebar and expand function features.**Considering project functionality**

I'm thinking about what would be functional and valuable for the project. Maybe I could list projects in the `/admin/projects/page.tsx`, but there's already a projects tab on the content page. Creating a dedicated analytics section might be helpful, but implementing CRUD pages for all content models seems like a lot. Instead, having just a dashboard with sections like content, media, analytics, and security could be enough. I'll consider maximizing user value with features like a premium homepage showcasing dynamic stats and a real admin dashboard.**Enhancing the homepage**

I'm considering adding a new section to the homepage, like an "Engagement Signal Bar" or "Operating Principles." However, the user asked for a full enhancement, and I realize broader UI changes could just come from upgrading existing sections with richer content and interactions. 

For the Work section, I could include features like category filters, metrics cards, and an active project detail panel. And for the Writing section, I wonder if making articles clickable or adding article drawers would be beneficial. Maybe I could also implement `/writing/[slug]` pages!**Creating dynamic content pages**

I’m thinking about how to enhance the site by creating dynamic article pages from the database and default content. Specifically, I see value in making project detail pages more immersive and functional since the "Read" button isn’t clickable now. I could implement `/writing/[slug]/page.tsx` for articles using `react-markdown` and `/work/[slug]/page.tsx` to showcase projects with all necessary information. Linking these properly could make the site much more engaging!**Optimizing data loading for dynamic pages**

I'm considering using the database to load titles and excerpts, but for long content, we might need a fallback. It seems like a good idea to create a file, `src/lib/site-data.ts`, that exports strongly typed data arrays for the public site. However, I wonder if the database pages will truly function without seeding it with these defaults. I think I could implement central data, dynamic routes, a newsletter, and an admin dashboard, which feels feasible. I'll also check if we need more dependencies for this setup!**Setting up testing with Vitest**

I’m considering using Vitest for simple Node tests, but I might also need `vite-tsconfig-paths` for TypeScript path aliasing. It looks like Bun’s test runner might work with `vitest` APIs, but I think it’s safer to just add `vitest` and its configuration. I wonder if using `bun test --preload` could work since my tests import `vitest`. It seems easiest to add `vitest` and run it with `bunx vitest run`, and I'll probably need to add a test script too!

content.thinking:
**Considering dynamic and static rendering**

I’m thinking about how server components can interact with the database at render time. During `next build`, dynamic rendering might not always execute as expected; static optimization could attempt pre-rendering unless dynamic features are active. To avoid needing the database at build time, I could use `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';` or adjust configurations for caching. Public pages might lean towards static defaults, while dynamic article pages could pull from a default content file, simplifying the process.**Exploring admin and content management**

I’m considering how admins can query the database on the server side while ensuring the user-facing website remains functional with content pages and CTAs. Having a central content file with rich data seems to enhance consistency and reality for users. It sounds like a solid plan to let public listings default to central content while dynamic article pages first check the database and then use defaults if necessary. This way, updated content can later integrate with existing pages. I think this approach is good enough!**Considering page view analytics**

I think I need to implement a page view analytics endpoint and tracking component. I can create a client component that generates a session ID on mount and sends a beacon with the necessary data to the analytics API endpoint. 

The admin analytics page would use this data model. While it might seem like a lot, I can also set up recent activity logging to capture valuable user actions and display this data on the dashboard. I'll check the database models for ActivityLog to ensure everything integrates smoothly.**Considering server improvements**

I’m thinking about how the admin page server fetches content data and sends it to the client, which seems like extra work. Maybe I should create an analytics page component to display actual metrics — Recharts could be useful for that. I also wonder if I should trim the sidebar to only what’s necessary if we’re not adding more. Implementing an analytics page sounds good, especially since we have page view data. A security page to show sessions could be useful too!**Planning package updates**

I could use the command `bun add -d vitest vite-tsconfig-paths` to modify the local project, but there's a medium risk involved. I need to confirm the package manager and whether I can use `bunx` to run the installed packages. It looks like `vitest` isn't installed yet, so I'll add it. Before making any edits, I should create a to-do list for updates. We already have some improvements identified, so I'll mark item 2 as completed and item 3 as in progress after getting the edits underway.

input.todos:
1. [completed] Audit the `final` app structure, scripts, and current UX/features
2. [completed] Identify the highest-impact improvements for functionality, performance, and security
3. [in_progress] Implement frontend/backend enhancements in `final`
4. [pending] Run validators and fix any issues until clean

message.openaiReasoningSummary:
**Considering dynamic and static rendering**

I’m thinking about how server components can interact with the database at render time. During `next build`, dynamic rendering might not always execute as expected; static optimization could attempt pre-rendering unless dynamic features are active. To avoid needing the database at build time, I could use `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';` or adjust configurations for caching. Public pages might lean towards static defaults, while dynamic article pages could pull from a default content file, simplifying the process.**Exploring admin and content management**

I’m considering how admins can query the database on the server side while ensuring the user-facing website remains functional with content pages and CTAs. Having a central content file with rich data seems to enhance consistency and reality for users. It sounds like a solid plan to let public listings default to central content while dynamic article pages first check the database and then use defaults if necessary. This way, updated content can later integrate with existing pages. I think this approach is good enough!**Considering page view analytics**

I think I need to implement a page view analytics endpoint and tracking component. I can create a client component that generates a session ID on mount and sends a beacon with the necessary data to the analytics API endpoint. 

The admin analytics page would use this data model. While it might seem like a lot, I can also set up recent activity logging to capture valuable user actions and display this data on the dashboard. I'll check the database models for ActivityLog to ensure everything integrates smoothly.**Considering server improvements**

I’m thinking about how the admin page server fetches content data and sends it to the client, which seems like extra work. Maybe I should create an analytics page component to display actual metrics — Recharts could be useful for that. I also wonder if I should trim the sidebar to only what’s necessary if we’re not adding more. Implementing an analytics page sounds good, especially since we have page view data. A security page to show sessions could be useful too!**Planning package updates**

I could use the command `bun add -d vitest vite-tsconfig-paths` to modify the local project, but there's a medium risk involved. I need to confirm the package manager and whether I can use `bunx` to run the installed packages. It looks like `vitest` isn't installed yet, so I'll add it. Before making any edits, I should create a to-do list for updates. We already have some improvements identified, so I'll mark item 2 as completed and item 3 as in progress after getting the edits underway.

1. [completed] Audit the `final` app structure, scripts, and current UX/features
2. [completed] Identify the highest-impact improvements for functionality, performance, and security
3. [in_progress] Implement frontend/backend enhancements in `final`
4. [pending] Run validators and fix any issues until clean

content.thinking:
**Updating security measures**

I’m considering keeping the current setup and creating a new asynchronous process for the newsletter/contact since the user emphasized security. I might explore updating the auth rate limits to use a generic memory fallback and make it asynchronous. This would involve modifying the route accordingly. I think it could be useful to make `rate-limit.ts` more generic so that `auth.ts` isn’t reliant on rate limit functions, even though tests currently cover them.**Considering code structure and safety features**

I'm thinking about potential functions for `src/lib/request.ts` that could simplify things, like getting the client IP, user agent, and origin from requests. I should probably check if these are included to help with requests.

For `src/lib/activity.ts`, I’m considering logging activities with a try-catch block for error handling when interacting with the database. Also, when setting up a contact route, I might want to add validation to ensure safety, including hidden fields for honeypot checks and possibly managing user input on the website field.**Evaluating page structure**

I’m thinking about the page structure and whether client state is necessary. The content and analytics pages may require some client features, so I can consider creating a server page with a client chart. I believe I can set up a dashboard page as a server component, a content page that fetches data on the client side, and have the analytics page as a server component with the chart as a client component. Security also needs a server component, and I might have to create a couple of new pages and routes.**Organizing updates and fixes**

I need to update authentication routes for contact, upload, and admin kinds. Then, I should add site content and possibly a content service. I'll also update public sections and create new routes along with admin pages and the sidebar. There are bugs to fix too, like the splash overlay and duplicate useEffect in the header. I should install vitest, configure it, and add tests — but maybe it’s best to do this in chunks to validate things incrementally!**Planning file updates**

I'm focusing on applying patches one file at a time, and I need to use absolute paths. I’ll start with utility files like `rate-limit.ts`, `request.ts`, `activity.ts`, and `site-content.ts`. I’m considering `content-service.ts` too, but it might be too large, so I need to decide if it’s necessary. I might also add `schemas.ts` or `validation.ts`. Crafting these files carefully is essential to ensure the right data shape.**Defining types and constants**

I'm defining several interfaces for my project. For example, `HeroMetric` includes a label, value, and detail; `SocialLink` has a label and href; while `ProjectCaseStudy` covers a range of elements like slug, title, and tech stack. Additionally, I've got constants like `siteProfile`, `heroMetrics`, and `featuredProjects`. I’m considering including `operatingModelStats` as well. For the content service, I should use default arrays from `site-content` to keep everything organized and functional.**Mapping database rows**

+    content: `# Building AI-Powered Workflows That Actually Work
+
+AI workflows fail for the same reason many software initiatives fail: the implementation is optimized for the demo instead of the operating environment.

+In real teams, workflows are judged by whether they reduce ambiguity, preserve quality, and create confidence under pressure. A beautiful flowchart means very little if the people running the process cannot explain why the system made a decision.

+## Start With Friction, Not Fantasy

+

+

+
+The answer is not to ignore architecture. It is to design for leverage with appropriate weight.
+
+## What Good Solo Systems Do
+
+A strong solo-founder system should do three things:
+
+1. reduce cognitive overhead,
+2. preserve delivery speed,
+3. make future complexity easier to absorb.
+
+If a process adds ceremony without leverage, it is probably too heavy.
+
+## Create Default Paths
+
+The easiest way to stay fast is to remove unnecessary decisions.
+
+Choose defaults for naming, release flow, bug triage, customer follow-up, and product feedback intake. Small defaults save enormous energy over time.
+
+## Protect the Feedback Loop
+
+Founders often overbuild before they have enough signal. Good system design keeps the path from user behavior to product decision as short as possible.
+
+That means lightweight analytics, visible metrics, and recurring review habits. A system that hides the truth is not helping.
+
+## Design for Recovery
+
+Things will break. Pages will regress. Customers will misunderstand what you meant. Build in recovery mechanisms:

+Reliability is not just about prevention. It is about graceful response.

+
+The goal is not to imitate a larger company. The goal is to build an operating system that lets one capable person move with unusual clarity.

+That is often enough to outperform much larger teams.`,

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /home/donovan/Projects/DoulgasMitchell.info/final/src/lib/forms.ts
+import { z } from 'zod';
+
+const trimmedString = (min: number, max: number) =>
+  z
+    .string()
+    .trim()
+    .min(min)
+    .max(max);
+
+export const contactSubmissionSchema = z.object({
+  name: trimmedString(2, 80),
+  email: z.string().trim().email().max(120),
+  subject: z.string().trim().max(120).optional().default(''),
+  message: trimmedString(20, 2000),
+  source: z.string().trim().max(60).optional().default('website'),
+  website: z.string().trim().max(120).optional().default(''),
+});
+
+export const newsletterSubscriptionSchema = z.object({
+  email: z.string().trim().email().max(120),
+  name: z.string().trim().max(80).optional().default(''),
+  source: z.string().trim().max(60).optional().default('website'),
+});
*** End Patch

**Installing vitest and prisma**

I need to install vitest and update the lock file, and it seems that using the command `bun install` might do the trick. I also need to consider that the Prisma client might need to be generated. Before running the command, I should check if the parent directory exists. Using an absolute path for the command seems best, like `bun install --cwd "/home/.../final"` if that option is supported. It does come with some risk since it modifies node_modules and the lock file.

content.thinking:
**Fixing Typecheck Issues**

I'm noticing several typecheck errors, some in files I haven't even edited, so I definitely need to address those. The tests require a `prisma generate`, and it turns out `getFileIcon('audio/mp3')` is throwing errors because the allowed audio list uses `audio/mpeg`. I should probably update the function to support `audio/mp3` as an alias. Adapting the `pretest` script might simplify running `prisma generate` beforehand, but since the user wants everything tested, I’ll ensure the generated client is included in the project to address these issues systematically.**Addressing Typecheck Errors**

I’m identifying typecheck errors across different categories. First, the `examples/websocket frontend.tsx/server.ts` files are missing necessary `socket.io` dependencies. I can either exclude examples from the typecheck or add those dependencies, as they may not be part of the main website. It might be better to update the `tsconfig` to exclude `examples/**/*`, or maybe just the specific websocket example. I'll also need to look into legacy component type errors, deciding whether to exclude them from compilation or fix them. I should inspect the relevant files in parallel.

**Fixing Type Errors**

I'm exploring ways to resolve some TypeScript configuration issues, like excluding `custom-cursor.tsx` if it's really not used elsewhere. I noticed that using `useRef<number | null>(null)` could streamline the fix for certain errors. I'll also look into patching the hero section, possibly using typed variants and conditional motion reduction. Additionally, I'm addressing cache and content service errors, along with type complaints in env.ts and auth.ts. It feels manageable, and I’ll inspect route usage if needed.**Patching Type Issues**

I’m considering patching the code to use `base64URLDecode` for credential IDs and encoding the `credentialID` string with `base64URLEncode`. While I could simplify this by excluding passkeys from type checks if they're not used, the user wants a secure approach, so I'll go ahead and patch it. I also need to convert types for `verifyRegistrationResponse` and `generateOptions` with `credentialIDs` as `Uint8Array`. There are type errors in `upload.ts`, and I should inspect specific lines to address those. Lastly, I’ll work on resolving `auth.test` failures due to `prisma generate`.**Improving Test Scripts**

I'm thinking about adding a `pretest` script or just running `prisma generate` within the validators. This could help make tests more reproducible. I'll consider updating the test script to run `prisma generate && vitest run`, but I realize that `prisma generate` can slow down every test run. So, I’ll settle on simplifying it to just the test script: "prisma generate && vitest run." That seems like a good balance!**Patching upload issues**

I’m running `bun run test` after installation, which is good. It seems like I need to patch the package.json. There's an issue with the upload test failing due to an MP3 alias. I could fix this by adding `'audio/mp3'` to the allowed types or adjusting how getFileCategory treats it. Since MP3 is common, I’ll patch several files, starting with env, auth, tsconfig, ASCII particles, the custom cursor, the hero-section, and upload. I might also inspect content-service lines near the errors.

{"success":true,"content":"// Douglas Mitchell Editorial Platform - Content Model
// Single source of truth for all content

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

// Core Content Models
model Article {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  slug        String   @unique
  title       String
  subtitle    String?
  excerpt     String
  content     String   // Markdown content
  coverImage  String?
  category    String   // essay, technical, insight, case-study
  tags        String   // JSON array of tags
  featured    Boolean  @default(false)
  published   Boolean  @default(false)
  publishedAt DateTime?
  readingTime Int      @default(5)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
  
  // Relations
  blocks      ArticleBlock[]
  media       ArticleMedia[]
  comments    Comment[]
  reactions   Reaction[]
  
  @@index([slug])
  @@index([category])
  @@index([published, featured])
}

model ArticleBlock {
  id        String  @id @default(cuid())
  articleId String
  article   Article @relation(fields: [articleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  blockType String  // text, quote, code, image, divider, ascii-art, evidence-grid
  content   String  // JSON content specific to block type
  order     Int
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([articleId, order])
}

model Project {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  slug          String   @unique
  title         String
  subtitle      String?
  description   String
  longDescription String?
  coverImage    String?
  githubUrl     String?
  liveUrl       String?
  techStack     String   // JSON array of technologies
  category      String   // ai-automation, web-development, system-design, open-source
  featured      Boolean  @default(false)
  status        String   @default("completed") // completed, in-progress, archived
  startDate     DateTime?
  endDate       DateTime?
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt

  media         ProjectMedia[]
  comments      Comment[]
  reactions     Reaction[]
  
  @@index([slug])
  @@index([category])
  @@index([featured])
}

model Certification {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  title       String
  issuer      String
  issuerLogo  String?
  credentialUrl String?
  issueDate   DateTime
  expiryDate  DateTime?
  credentialId String?
  description String?
  skills      String   // JSON array of skills
  featured    Boolean  @default(false)
  order       Int      @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([featured, order])
}

model Book {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  title         String
  subtitle      String?
  description   String
  coverImage    String?
  amazonUrl     String?
  publisher     String?
  publishDate   DateTime?
  isbn          String?
  pages         Int?
  featured      Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt
}

model SiteConfig {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  key         String   @unique
  value       String   // JSON value
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
}

model ContactSubmission {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  name      String
  email     String
  subject   String?
  message   String
  source    String?  // Where the submission came from
  status    String   @default("new") // new, read, responded, archived
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([status, createdAt])
}

// ============================================
// AUTHENTICATION & AUTHORIZATION
// ============================================

model AdminUser {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  email        String   @unique
  name         String?
  passwordHash String?
  role         String   @default("admin") // admin, editor
  avatar       String?
  bio          String?
  lastLoginAt  DateTime?
  isActive     Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt
  
  // Relations
  sessions     Session[]
  passkeys     PasskeyCredential[]
  activityLogs ActivityLog[]
  
  @@index([email])
  @@index([isActive])
}

model Session {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  token     String   @unique
  userId    String
  user      AdminUser @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  expiresAt DateTime
  ipAddress String?
  userAgent String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([token])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([expiresAt])
}

model PasskeyCredential {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  credentialId String  @unique
  userId      String
  user        AdminUser @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  publicKey   Bytes
  counter     Int      @default(0)
  deviceType  String?  // platform, cross-platform
  deviceName  String?  // e.g., "MacBook Pro Touch ID"
  lastUsedAt  DateTime?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([credentialId])
}

// ============================================
// MEDIA MANAGEMENT
// ============================================

model Media {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  filename      String   @unique
  originalName  String
  mimeType      String
  category      String   // image, video, audio, document
  size          Int
  width         Int?     // for images/videos
  height        Int?     // for images/videos
  duration      Int?     // for videos/audio (seconds)
  url           String
  thumbnailUrl  String?
  alt           String?  // accessibility text
  caption       String?
  description   String?
  tags          String?  // JSON array
  folder        String   @default("uncategorized")
  uploadedById  String?
  isPublic      Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt
  
  // Relations
  articleUsages ArticleMedia[]
  projectUsages ProjectMedia[]
  
  @@index([category])
  @@index([folder])
  @@index([mimeType])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

// Many-to-many: Articles <-> Media
model ArticleMedia {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  articleId String
  article   Article  @relation(fields: [articleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  mediaId   String
  media     Media    @relation(fields: [mediaId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  order     Int      @default(0)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@unique([articleId, mediaId])
  @@index([articleId])
  @@index([mediaId])
}

// Many-to-many: Projects <-> Media
model ProjectMedia {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  projectId String
  project   Project  @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  mediaId   String
  media     Media    @relation(fields: [mediaId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  order     Int      @default(0)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@unique([projectId, mediaId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([mediaId])
}

// ============================================
// SOCIAL FEATURES
// ============================================

model Comment {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  content     String
  authorName  String
  authorEmail String
  authorUrl   String?
  articleId   String?
  article     Article? @relation(fields: [articleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  projectId   String?
  project     Project? @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  parentId    String?
  parent      Comment?  @relation("CommentReplies", fields: [parentId], references: [id])
  replies     Comment[] @relation("CommentReplies")
  isApproved  Boolean   @default(false)
  isSpam      Boolean   @default(false)
  likes       Int       @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime  @updatedAt
  
  @@index([articleId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([isApproved, isSpam])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

model Reaction {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  type      String   // like, love, applaud, insightful
  articleId String?
  article   Article? @relation(fields: [articleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  projectId String?
  project   Project? @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  sessionId String   // anonymous tracking
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@unique([articleId, projectId, sessionId, type])
  @@index([articleId])
  @@index([projectId])
}

model Newsletter {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  email     String   @unique
  name      String?
  isActive  Boolean  @default(true)
  confirmedAt DateTime?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([email])
  @@index([isActive])
}

// ============================================
// ANALYTICS & LOGGING
// ============================================

model ActivityLog {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  action     String   // create, update, delete, login, logout, publish, etc.
  resource   String   // article, project, media, user, etc.
  resourceId String?
  details    String?  // JSON string with additional data
  userId     String?
  user       AdminUser? @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  ipAddress  String?
  userAgent  String?
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([action])
  @@index([resource])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

model PageView {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  path        String
  referrer    String?
  userAgent   String?
  ipAddress   String?
  country     String?
  city        String?
  sessionId   String
  duration    Int?     // seconds spent on page
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([path])
  @@index([sessionId])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

// ============================================
// LAYOUT & THEMING
// ============================================

model LayoutBlock {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  name        String   @unique
  type        String   // hero, about, work, contact, custom
  isActive    Boolean  @default(true)
  order       Int      @default(0)
  settings    String   // JSON configuration
  content     String   // JSON content blocks
  styles      String?  // Custom CSS
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
  
  @@index([type])
  @@index([isActive, order])
}

model Setting {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  key         String   @unique
  value       String   // JSON value
  type        String   @default("string") // string, number, boolean, json
  category    String   @default("general") // general, seo, social, analytics
  isPublic    Boolean  @default(false) // expose to frontend?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
  
  @@index([key])
  @@index([category])
}

This file can house pure functions and constants for things like `ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES` and `validateFileSize`. # Done

I upgraded `final/` into a much more real, production-ready portfolio: dynamic homepage content, new `/writing/[slug]` and `/work/[slug]` detail pages, working newsletter signup, page-view analytics, hardened contact/upload/auth flows, and real admin dashboard/content/analytics/security pages.  

I also fixed core platform issues: Prisma schema/client generation, passkey challenge handling, client/server upload separation, security headers, sitemap generation, and removed build-time/type issues.  

## Validation

Passed successfully:

- `bun run lint`
- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run test`
- `bun run build`

I did not push to GitHub because you did not explicitly ask me to run `git push`.

- I fixed Prisma schema issues, enabled real validators, and ensured all tests passed. 
- Note that I didn't push to GitHub since the user didn't specifically request it.

content.thinking:
**Reviewing git changes**

Before any git commit or push, I should run 'git diff --cached' to check all the changes that are about to be committed, and 'git status' to confirm what’s included. I need to examine the diff for any secrets and stop if any are detected, even if I believe there are none. I also need to create a commit message based on the changes, since the user didn’t provide one. There's also a need to think about the push to remote, which requires clear user instructions.**Analyzing package changes**

I recently ran `bun install`, which likely updated the `bun.lock`, and I'm wondering if the `package-lock` might have changed too. I should check the status and run `git status`, `git diff`, and `git log` in parallel since a commit is requested. It might be low risk to do this from the repo root. I need to ensure I'm in the correct directory and consider updating TodoWrite while analyzing those git commands simultaneously. Using absolute paths with `cd` or `git -C` could be a safer option.

@@ -4,507 +4,53 @@ This file provides guidance to AI coding agents when working with code in this r
 
 ## Project Overview
 
-This is **Senpai's Isekai** (douglasmitchell.info) - a modern personal blog and portfolio website for Douglas D. Mitchell, featuring sophisticated animations, database-backed content management, and a secure admin portal.
+**Senpai's Isekai** (douglasmitchell.info) - Next.js 16 + React 19 + TypeScript personal blog/portfolio with PostgreSQL/Prisma, JWT auth, and admin portal.
 
-The website showcases:
-- A photography gallery organized into three series (Recent Post, Tech Deck, Project)
-- A journal/blog with markdown content
-- Contact forms and newsletter signup
-- Secure admin portal for content management
-- Rich animations and glassmorphism design
+## Non-Obvious Patterns
 
-## Technology Stack
+### Middleware & Error Handling
+- API routes MUST use `withMiddleware()` wrapper from `@/lib/middleware`
+- Use custom error classes: `ValidationError`, `AuthenticationError`, `NotFoundError`, `AuthorizationError`, `RateLimitError`
+- Return responses via `successResponse()`, `errorResponse()`, `paginatedResponse()`
+- Input validation via Zod schemas in `src/lib/validations.ts`
 
-- **Framework**: Next.js 16 (App Router)
-- **Runtime**: React 19 + TypeScript 5
-- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4 + CSS Variables
-- **UI Components**: shadcn/ui (New York style) with Radix primitives
-- **Animations**: Framer Motion
-- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
-- **State Management**: Zustand (client-side)
-- **Authentication**: JWT tokens in httpOnly cookies + bcrypt + WebAuthn/Passkeys
-- **Package Manager**: Bun (also supports npm)
-- **Deployment**: Standalone output for self-hosting or Vercel
+### Data & State
+- Static fallback data in `src/lib/data.ts` used when database is empty
+- Use `cn()` utility from `src/lib/utils.ts` for conditional classes (not clsx directly)
+- Zustand stores in `src/lib/store.ts` for client-side state (lightbox, reactions, admin)
 
-## Build and Development Commands
+### Database
+- Gallery has 3 series: `recent-post`, `tech-deck`, `project`
+- Images uploaded to `/public/uploads/images/`, served statically
+- Admin password via `ADMIN_PASSWORD` env var - no default, will fail without it
 
-```bash
-# Install dependencies
-bun install
-
-# Development server (port 3000, logs to dev.log)
-bun run dev
-
-# Production build (includes Prisma generation and post-build standalone setup)
-bun run build
-
-# Production server (run from build output)
-bun run start
-
-# Linting
-bun run lint
-
-# Database operations
-bun run db:push      # Push schema changes to database
-bun run db:generate  # Generate Prisma client
-bun run db:migrate   # Run migrations
-bun run db:reset     # Reset database
-bun run db:seed      # Seed database with initial data
-```
-
-## Project Structure
-
-```
-src/
-├── app/                    # Next.js App Router pages and API routes
-│   ├── api/               # API endpoints
-│   │   ├── admin/         # Admin authentication & passkey management
-│   │   ├── activity/      # Activity log endpoints
-│   │   ├── contact/       # Contact form submission
-│   │   ├── gallery/       # Gallery CRUD + batch operations
-│   │   ├── journal/       # Journal CRUD
-│   │   ├── layout/        # Layout block management
-│   │   ├── newsletter/    # Newsletter subscription
-│   │   ├── settings/      # Site settings
-│   │   └── upload/        # Image upload handling
-│   ├── admin/             # Admin portal page (~2900 lines)
-│   ├── about/             # About page
-│   ├── contact/           # Contact page
-│   ├── events/            # Events page
-│   ├── faq/               # FAQ page
-│   ├── galleries/         # Gallery showcase page
-│   ├── journal/           # Journal/blog page
-│   ├── press-kit/         # Press kit page
-│   ├── samples/           # Samples page
-│   ├── globals.css        # Global styles + design system
-│   ├── layout.tsx         # Root layout with fonts
-│   └── page.tsx           # Landing page
-├── components/            # React components
-│   ├── ui/               # shadcn/ui primitives (50+ components)
-│   ├── animations.tsx    # Animation components (ScrollReveal, Magnetic, etc.)
-│   ├── contact-form.tsx  # Contact form component

-
-## Code Style Guidelines
-
-### General Principles
-
-- **No unnecessary comments** - Avoid adding comments unless explicitly requested
-- **Prefer named exports** for functions and components
-- **Use TypeScript** for all files - no plain JavaScript
-- **Avoid `any`** - Use proper types or `unknown` when necessary
-- **Use functional components** with hooks - no class components
-- **Prefer `const`** over `let` - only use `let` when reassignment is truly needed
-- **Prefer string literals** over enums where possible
-
-### Imports
-
-- **Sort imports** logically: external libs → internal modules → relative paths
-- **Use `type` keyword** for type-only imports to improve performance
-- **Avoid barrel imports** (index files) - import directly from modules
-
-```typescript
-// Good
-import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
-import { getCorsHeaders, logRequest } from '@/lib/security';
-import type { CreateGalleryImageInput } from '@/lib/validations';
-
-// Client components
-"use client";
-import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from "react";
-import { motion } from "framer-motion";
-```
-
-### File Naming
-
-- **Components**: PascalCase (e.g., `PageTransition.tsx`, `GalleryGrid.tsx`)
-- **Utilities/Modules**: camelCase (e.g., `utils.ts`, `security.ts`, `store.ts`)
-- **Types**: PascalCase with `.types.ts` suffix or co-located with `type` keyword
-- **Constants**: PascalCase for exported constants, camelCase for internal
-
-### Component Patterns
-
-- **Props interface** should be defined inline for small components, or above the component
-- **Use defaults** for optional props in function parameters
-- **Extract complex types** to shared types file if reused across multiple components
-
-```typescript
-// Good component pattern
-interface PageTransitionProps {
-  children: ReactNode;
-  className?: string;
-  variant?: "fade" | "slide" | "scale";
-}
-
-export function PageTransition({ 
-  children, 
-  className = "",
-  variant = "slide" 
-}: PageTransitionProps) {
-  // Component implementation
-}
-```
-
-### React Hooks
-
-- **Extract custom hooks** to `src/hooks/` or component file
-- **Use `useCallback`** for event handlers passed to memoized components
-- **Use `useMemo`** for expensive calculations
-- **Always include dependencies** in useEffect (lint rule is disabled but prefer correctness)
-- **Check for SSR** when using browser APIs (e.g., `typeof window !== 'undefined'`)
-
-```typescript
-// Good hook usage
-const handleMouseMove = useCallback((e: React.MouseEvent) => {
-  if (prefersReducedMotion || disabled || !ref.current) return;
-  // ...
-}, [prefersReducedMotion, disabled, strength]);
-
-const items = useMemo(() => {
-  return data.filter(item => item.active);
-}, [data]);
-```
-
-### API Routes
-
-- **Wrap handlers** with `withMiddleware()` for consistent error handling, CORS, logging
-- **Use named handlers** (GET, POST, etc.)
-- **Validate input** using Zod schemas from `validations.ts`
-- **Use error classes** from middleware: `ValidationError`, `AuthenticationError`, `NotFoundError`, etc.
-- **Return responses** using `successResponse()`, `errorResponse()`, or `paginatedResponse()`
-
-```typescript
-// Good API route pattern
-import { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
-import { withMiddleware } from '@/lib/middleware';
-import { validateInput, successResponse, NotFoundError } from '@/lib/middleware';
-import { createGalleryImageSchema } from '@/lib/validations';
-
-export const GET = withMiddleware(async (request: NextRequest) => {
-  const body = await request.json();
-  const data = validateInput(createGalleryImageSchema, body);
-  
-  // ... implementation
-  
-  return successResponse(image);
-});
-```
-
-### Error Handling
-
-- **Use custom error classes** for different error types (see `middleware.ts`)
-- **Log errors** appropriately with `console.error`
-- **Return structured errors** with `errorResponse()`
-
-```typescript
-// Throwing errors
-throw new ValidationError('Invalid input', { field: 'email' });
-throw new AuthenticationError();
-throw new NotFoundError('Image');
-```
-
-### Zod Schemas
-
-- **Define schemas** in `src/lib/validations.ts`
-- **Export types** using `z.infer<typeof schemaName>`
-- **Use descriptive error messages** in schema definitions
-- **Chain validators** for complex validation
-
-```typescript
-// Good schema pattern
-export const createGalleryImageSchema = z.object({
-  src: z.string().url('Invalid image URL').or(z.string().startsWith('/images/')),
-  alt: z.string().min(1, 'Alt text is required').max(200),
-  series: z.enum(['recent-post', 'tech-deck', 'project']),
-});
-
-export type CreateGalleryImageInput = z.infer<typeof createGalleryImageSchema>;
-```
-
-### CSS / Tailwind
-
-- **Use Tailwind** for all styling - no custom CSS files unless necessary
-- **Use `cn()` utility** from `utils.ts` for conditional classes
-- **Use CSS variables** for theming (defined in `globals.css`)
-- **Design system**: Cream white + Glassmorphism + Soft warm gray
-
-```typescript
-// Good className usage
-className={cn(
-  "base-styles",
-  isActive && "active-styles",
-  variant === "primary" ? "bg-primary" : "bg-secondary"
-)}
-```
-
-### Accessibility
-
-- **Support reduced motion** - check `useReducedMotion()` preference
-- **Use semantic HTML** elements
-- **Include proper ARIA attributes** when needed
-- **Focus visible styles** are defined in `globals.css`
-
-## Key Systems
-
-### Authentication Flow
-
-1. **Login**: POST to `/api/admin/auth` with password
-2. **JWT Generation**: Token stored in `admin-session` httpOnly cookie
-3. **Session Storage**: Session also recorded in database with expiration
-4. **Validation**: Each API request validates both JWT signature and database session
-5. **Passkeys**: WebAuthn support for passwordless authentication (optional)
-6. **Rate Limiting**: 5 requests per 60 seconds per IP (configurable via env vars)
-
-### Gallery System
-
-- **Three Series**: `recent-post`, `tech-deck`, `project`

-import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
-import type { NextRequest } from "next/server";
-
-const SECURITY_HEADERS: Record<string, string> = {
-  "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
-  "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
-  "Referrer-Policy": "strict-origin-when-cross-origin",
-  "Permissions-Policy": "camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()",
-  "Strict-Transport-Security": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload",
-  "Content-Security-Policy": [
-    "default-src 'self'",
-    "base-uri 'self'",
-    "form-action 'self'",
-    "frame-ancestors 'none'",
-    "img-src 'self' data: blob: https:",
-    "style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com",
-    "font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com data:",
-    "script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'",
-    "connect-src 'self' https:",
-  ].join('; '),
-};
-
-function applySecurityHeaders(response: NextResponse): NextResponse {
-  for (const [header, value] of Object.entries(SECURITY_HEADERS)) {
-    response.headers.set(header, value);
-  }
-
-  return response;
-}
-
-const CANONICAL_HOST = (
-  process.env.CANONICAL_HOST || "www.douglasmitchell.info"
-).toLowerCase();
-
-function buildCspHeader(): string {
-  return [
-    "default-src 'self'",
-    "base-uri 'self'",
-    "frame-ancestors 'none'",
-    "img-src 'self' data: blob: https:",
-    "font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com data:",
-    "style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com",
-    "script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'",
-    "connect-src 'self' https:",
-    "object-src 'none'",
-    "form-action 'self'",
-    "upgrade-insecure-requests",
-  ].join('; ');
-}
-
-function withSecurityHeaders(response: NextResponse): NextResponse {
-  response.headers.set('Content-Security-Policy', buildCspHeader());
-  response.headers.set('Referrer-Policy', 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin');
-  response.headers.set('X-Content-Type-Options', 'nosniff');
-  response.headers.set('X-Frame-Options', 'DENY');
-  response.headers.set('Permissions-Policy', 'camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()');
-
-  if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') {
-    response.headers.set('Strict-Transport-Security', 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload');
-  }
-
-  return response;
-}
-
-function shouldRedirectToCanonicalHost(host: string): boolean {
-  const normalizedHost = host.toLowerCase();
-  const canonicalVariant = CANONICAL_HOST.startsWith("www.")
-    ? CANONICAL_HOST.replace(/^www\./, "")
-    : `www.${CANONICAL_HOST}`;
-
-  if (normalizedHost === CANONICAL_HOST) {
-    return false;
-  }
-
-  if (normalizedHost === "localhost:3000" || normalizedHost === "127.0.0.1:3000") {
-    return false;
-  }
-
-  if (normalizedHost === canonicalVariant) {
-    return true;
-  }
-
-  return normalizedHost.endsWith(".vercel.app");
-}
-
-export function proxy(request: NextRequest): NextResponse {
-  if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production") {
-    return applySecurityHeaders(NextResponse.next());
-  }
-
-  const host = request.headers.get("host");
-  if (!host || !shouldRedirectToCanonicalHost(host)) {
-    return applySecurityHeaders(NextResponse.next());
-  }
-
-  const redirectUrl = request.nextUrl.clone();
-  redirectUrl.protocol = "https";
-  redirectUrl.host = CANONICAL_HOST;
-
-  return applySecurityHeaders(NextResponse.redirect(redirectUrl, 308));
-}
-
-export const config = {
-  matcher: ["/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|robots.txt|sitemap.xml).*)"],
-};

**Reviewing git changes**

I'm considering how to properly review all the changes being committed. The instructions suggest using `git diff --cached`, and I think I could try `git diff --cached --stat` for a summary, but the full diff can be quite large. Maybe using `git diff --cached --name-only` alongside excluding large files would be helpful too. I should also keep in mind the output limit of 40k, so I might need to truncate. Using Grep could help me search for specific patterns in the staged files.**Considering Git Commands**

I'm thinking about the best way to review my changes. Running `git diff --cached -- final` might work, but that final folder has many files, so it could be a lot. I could run `git diff --cached --stat` and grep for specific patterns to manage it better. My developer suggested reviewing all changes with `git diff --cached`. I'll also check `git status` and my current branch before pushing. Drafting a commit message that aligns with previous ones is essential, like “feat: add premium final portfolio experience.”**Examining Git Status**

I should start by using `git status` to get a full picture, including the branch information. Running `git -C repo branch --show-current` will give me the current branch, and checking with `git -C repo remote -v` will confirm the remote setup. After that, I’ll focus on staging only the final changes and double-check the status again. That way, I can ensure everything is set up correctly before proceeding.

+#!/bin/bash
+
+# 将 stderr 重定向到 stdout，避免 execute_command 因为 stderr 输出而报错
+exec 2>&1
+
+set -e
+
+# 获取脚本所在目录（.zscripts 目录，即 workspace-agent/.zscripts）
+# 使用 $0 获取脚本路径（兼容 sh 和 bash）
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
+
+# Next.js 项目路径
+NEXTJS_PROJECT_DIR="/home/z/my-project"
+
+# 检查 Next.js 项目目录是否存在
+if [ ! -d "$NEXTJS_PROJECT_DIR" ]; then
+    echo "❌ 错误: Next.js 项目目录不存在: $NEXTJS_PROJECT_DIR"
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "🚀 开始构建 Next.js 应用和 mini-services..."
+echo "📁 Next.js 项目路径: $NEXTJS_PROJECT_DIR"
+
+# 切换到 Next.js 项目目录
+cd "$NEXTJS_PROJECT_DIR" || exit 1
+
+# 设置环境变量
+export NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
+
+BUILD_DIR="/tmp/build_fullstack_$BUILD_ID"
+echo "📁 清理并创建构建目录: $BUILD_DIR"
+mkdir -p "$BUILD_DIR"
+
+# 安装依赖
+echo "📦 安装依赖..."
+bun install
+
+# 构建 Next.js 应用
+echo "🔨 构建 Next.js 应用..."
+bun run build
+
+# 构建 mini-services
+# 检查 Next.js 项目目录下是否有 mini-services 目录
+if [ -d "$NEXTJS_PROJECT_DIR/mini-services" ]; then
+    echo "🔨 构建 mini-services..."
+    # 使用 workspace-agent 目录下的 mini-services 脚本
+    sh "$SCRIPT_DIR/mini-services-install.sh"
+    sh "$SCRIPT_DIR/mini-services-build.sh"
+
+    # 复制 mini-services-start.sh 到 mini-services-dist 目录
+    echo "  - 复制 mini-services-start.sh 到 $BUILD_DIR"
+    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/mini-services-start.sh" "$BUILD_DIR/mini-services-start.sh"
+    chmod +x "$BUILD_DIR/mini-services-start.sh"
+else
+    echo "ℹ️  mini-services 目录不存在，跳过"
+fi
+
+# 将所有构建产物复制到临时构建目录
+echo "📦 收集构建产物到 $BUILD_DIR..."
+
+# 复制 Next.js standalone 构建输出
+if [ -d ".next/standalone" ]; then
+    echo "  - 复制 .next/standalone"
+    cp -r .next/standalone "$BUILD_DIR/next-service-dist/"
+fi
+
+# 复制 Next.js 静态文件
+if [ -d ".next/static" ]; then
+    echo "  - 复制 .next/static"
+    mkdir -p "$BUILD_DIR/next-service-dist/.next"
+    cp -r .next/static "$BUILD_DIR/next-service-dist/.next/"
+fi
+
+# 复制 public 目录
+if [ -d "public" ]; then
+    echo "  - 复制 public"
+    cp -r public "$BUILD_DIR/next-service-dist/"
+fi
+
+# 最后再迁移数据库到 BUILD_DIR/db
+if [ "$(ls -A ./db 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
+    echo "🗄️  检测到数据库文件，运行数据库迁移..."
+    DATABASE_URL=file:$BUILD_DIR/db/custom.db bun run db:push
+    echo "✅ 数据库迁移完成"
+    ls -lah $BUILD_DIR/db
+else
+    echo "ℹ️  db 目录为空，跳过数据库迁移"
+fi
+
+# 复制 Caddyfile（如果存在）
+if [ -f "Caddyfile" ]; then
+    echo "  - 复制 Caddyfile"
+    cp Caddyfile "$BUILD_DIR/"
+else
+    echo "ℹ️  Caddyfile 不存在，跳过"
+fi
+
+# 复制 start.sh 脚本
+echo "  - 复制 start.sh 到 $BUILD_DIR"
+cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/start.sh" "$BUILD_DIR/start.sh"
+chmod +x "$BUILD_DIR/start.sh"
+
+# 打包到 $BUILD_DIR.tar.gz
+PACKAGE_FILE="${BUILD_DIR}.tar.gz"
+echo ""
+echo "📦 打包构建产物到 $PACKAGE_FILE..."
+cd "$BUILD_DIR" || exit 1
+tar -czf "$PACKAGE_FILE" .
+cd - > /dev/null || exit 1
+
+# # 清理临时目录
+# rm -rf "$BUILD_DIR"
+
+echo ""
+echo "✅ 构建完成！所有产物已打包到 $PACKAGE_FILE"
+echo "📊 打包文件大小:"
+ls -lh "$PACKAGE_FILE"

+#!/bin/bash
+
+# 配置项
+ROOT_DIR="/home/z/my-project/mini-services"
+DIST_DIR="/tmp/build_fullstack_$BUILD_ID/mini-services-dist"
+
+main() {
+    echo "🚀 开始批量构建..."
+    
+    # 检查 rootdir 是否存在
+    if [ ! -d "$ROOT_DIR" ]; then
+        echo "ℹ️  目录 $ROOT_DIR 不存在，跳过构建"
+        return
+    fi
+    
+    # 创建输出目录（如果不存在）
+    mkdir -p "$DIST_DIR"
+    
+    # 统计变量
+    success_count=0
+    fail_count=0
+    
+    # 遍历 mini-services 目录下的所有文件夹
+    for dir in "$ROOT_DIR"/*; do
+        # 检查是否是目录且包含 package.json
+        if [ -d "$dir" ] && [ -f "$dir/package.json" ]; then
+            project_name=$(basename "$dir")
+            
+            # 智能查找入口文件 (按优先级查找)
+            entry_path=""
+            for entry in "src/index.ts" "index.ts" "src/index.js" "index.js"; do
+                if [ -f "$dir/$entry" ]; then
+                    entry_path="$dir/$entry"
+                    break
+                fi
+            done
+            
+            if [ -z "$entry_path" ]; then
+                echo "⚠️  跳过 $project_name: 未找到入口文件 (index.ts/js)"
+                continue
+            fi
+            
+            echo ""
+            echo "📦 正在构建: $project_name..."
+            
+            # 使用 bun build CLI 构建
+            output_file="$DIST_DIR/mini-service-$project_name.js"
+            
+            if bun build "$entry_path" \
+                --outfile "$output_file" \
+                --target bun \
+                --minify; then
+                echo "✅ $project_name 构建成功 -> $output_file"
+                success_count=$((success_count + 1))
+            else
+                echo "❌ $project_name 构建失败"
+                fail_count=$((fail_count + 1))
+            fi
+        fi
+    done
+    
+    if [ -f ./.zscripts/mini-services-start.sh ]; then
+        cp ./.zscripts/mini-services-start.sh "$DIST_DIR/mini-services-start.sh"
+        chmod +x "$DIST_DIR/mini-services-start.sh"
+    fi
+    
+    echo ""
+    echo "🎉 所有任务完成！"
+    if [ $success_count -gt 0 ] || [ $fail_count -gt 0 ]; then
+        echo "✅ 成功: $success_count 个"
+        if [ $fail_count -gt 0 ]; then
+            echo "❌ 失败: $fail_count 个"
+        fi
+    fi
+}
+
+main
+
